items.criterion: admin_payment_intents table exists in sql/admin_payment_intents.sql with status state machine, message cursor fields, and proper indexes
db_handler has CRUD methods for admin_payment_intents: create, get by ID, get active for recipient (identity-gated lookup), list active, update
PaymentService.request_payment accepts optional amount_token parameter and creates payment with direct SOL amount when provided (amount_usd=None, token_price_usd=None)
initiate_payment tool appears in TOOLS list, ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS set, and the assertion at tools.py:916 passes
initiate_payment accepts amount_sol (not amount_usd) and does NOT accept wallet_address
When recipient has wallet on file: initiate_payment creates persisted intent with status=awaiting_test and starts test payment with producer='admin_chat'
When no wallet on file: initiate_payment creates persisted intent with status=awaiting_wallet, sends channel ping, stores prompt_message_id
initiate_payment fails closed when called from DM (no guild_id/source_channel_id)
initiate_payment fails closed on invalid recipient_user_id or amount_sol <= 0
Duplicate active intent for same (guild, channel, recipient) is prevented and returns existing intent info
Reply interceptor fires before _is_directed_at_bot and _can_user_message_bot gates in on_message
Intent matching is deterministic: (guild_id, channel_id, author_id) lookup returns single active intent before any agent classification
Agent classification only runs after single candidate intent is identified, categorizing reply as wallet_provided/positive_confirmation/declined/ambiguous/suspicious
Ambiguous, suspicious, or out-of-ordinary reply patterns fail closed and tag admin for review
Valid wallet reply triggers upsert_wallet, updates intent status+cursor, starts test payment
handle_payment_result for test-confirmed creates final payment with amount_token (direct SOL, no USD conversion) and posts both PaymentConfirmView button and free-text confirmation prompt
handle_payment_result for test-failed updates intent to failed and does NOT create final payment
Free-text positive confirmation calls confirm_payment with confirmed_by='free_text' and updates intent
PaymentConfirmView button confirmation also works as secondary path (existing button handler, no changes needed)
producer_ref includes timestamp to satisfy unique index on (producer, producer_ref, is_test)
source_channel_id injected in agent.py using key that does not conflict with existing tools
Startup reconciliation in cog_load queries active intents, scans channel history from last_scanned_message_id, processes missed replies
Intent status and message cursors are persisted in DB (survives restart)
All existing tests pass: test_scheduler.py, test_social_route_tools.py, full suite
System prompt documents initiate_payment with amount_sol and guild-channel requirement

New test file covers: both wallet paths, validation, duplicate prevention, wallet reply handling, agent classification, terminal callbacks, confirmation, concurrent intents, startup reconciliation, amount_token parameter
Multiple concurrent intents in same channel for different recipients handled correctly
execute_initiate_payment and _start_admin_payment_flow are each under ~80 lines
handle_payment_result follows GrantsCog structure: guard then branch is_test then branch status
_check_pending_payment_reply has minimal overhead for non-matching messages (single DB query on indexed columns)
Race-condition guard prevents double-processing of same intent reply
Agent classification prompt is concise and deterministic with clear category definitions
Manual smoke test: admin says 'pay @user 5 SOL' in channel, bot pings user, user replies wallet, test queues, confirmation button appears, user replies 'yes', payout executes

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The approved user note for v1 explicitly says not to require `wallet_address` in the admin tool call and to continue only from the intended recipient's wallet reply in the originating channel/thread, but the plan still centers the feature on an optional `wallet_address` parameter and a fail-if-missing path. That is a direct divergence from the accepted product requirement, not just an implementation choice.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Complete plan redesign to match the approved v1 user requirement. Removed the `wallet_address` parameter from the tool schema. Added in-channel wallet collection: when no wallet is on file, the tool creates a pending payment intent (in-memory on AdminChatCog), pings the recipient in the originating channel, and a new wallet-reply handler at the top of `on_message` intercepts the recipient's reply before the `_can_user_message_bot` permission gate. Added `source_channel_id` injection in agent.py so the tool knows which channel the admin sent the message from. Scoped the tool to guild channels only (admin DMs fail-closed since there's no guild context or channel to ping in). Added a dedicated test file `tests/test_admin_payments.py` with 8 test cases covering both paths, validation, wallet replies, and terminal callbacks. Clarified that the final-payment confirmation uses the existing in-channel PaymentConfirmView button (recipient clicks in the originating channel), and that `handle_payment_result` posts producer-specific notifications distinct from PaymentCog's generic notifications.

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan keeps the final payment on the existing confirmation-button flow, which is at least in-channel rather than DM, but it never adds the equally explicit requirement to ping the recipient in the source channel/thread and resume only from that recipient's follow-up message. The core recipient-driven continuation path is therefore still missing from the proposed work.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The new button-confirmation story is not fully consistent with the persisted intent state machine. `PaymentConfirmView` currently only calls `PaymentService.confirm_payment()` on the payment row, while the plan says button confirmation can remain unchanged as a secondary path. That leaves the admin intent stuck at `awaiting_confirmation` when the user clicks the button and the final payment moves to `queued`/`processing`, so restart recovery would keep treating that intent as still waiting for a reply and may re-scan or re-prompt unnecessarily.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The new button-confirmation story is not fully consistent with the persisted intent state machine. `PaymentConfirmView` currently only calls `PaymentService.confirm_payment()` on the payment row, while the plan says button confirmation can remain unchanged as a secondary path. That leaves the admin intent stuck at `awaiting_confirmation` when the user clicks the button and the final payment moves to `queued`/`processing`, so restart recovery would keep treating that intent as still waiting for a reply and may re-scan or re-prompt unnecessarily.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The closest existing flow is not just `request_payment()` plus a callback; `GrantsCog` also owns the surrounding state machine that waits for the applicant's wallet reply in `on_message`, validates it, and only then starts payments. That means the admin-payment problem is broader than one new tool executor and one handoff callback, because the repository already solves 'wait for user wallet input in-channel' as a cog-level workflow.

The plan fixes the admin-initiated half of the feature but does not cover how a normal member's wallet reply or follow-up confirmation request re-enters the system when admin chat currently treats member access as a gated capability.

Without persisted or at least explicitly tracked pending state keyed by guild/channel/recipient, there is nowhere to remember 'admin asked to pay user X, waiting for wallet reply in channel Y' after the tool returns, so the required async continuation path has no supporting infrastructure.

unresolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: A new caller mismatch appears in the reply-classification path: `_check_pending_payment_reply()` is planned to run before the existing `self._ensure_agent()` call in `AdminChatCog.on_message()`, but the classification logic depends on the Anthropic client currently created inside `AdminChatAgent`. After a restart, or any time the interceptor fires before a normal agent turn, `self.agent` can still be `None`, and the plan does not say where that client is initialized for interceptor/recovery callers.
unresolved_flags.evidence: A new caller mismatch appears in the reply-classification path: `_check_pending_payment_reply()` is planned to run before the existing `self._ensure_agent()` call in `AdminChatCog.on_message()`, but the classification logic depends on the Anthropic client currently created inside `AdminChatAgent`. After a restart, or any time the interceptor fires before a normal agent turn, `self.agent` can still be `None`, and the plan does not say where that client is initialized for interceptor/recovery callers.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Several `must` criteria validate the discarded `wallet_address` design rather than the approved v1 behavior, including the success case that explicitly requires `wallet_address` and the fail-closed case that errors when no wallet is on file. Those criteria are crisp, but they are measuring the wrong product requirement.

Without specifying whether the reviewed change should produce one shared notify message or an additional producer-specific message somewhere else, that criterion is not cleanly verifiable.

unresolved_flags.concern: Admin payments: Secondary button confirmation is not integrated with the new `admin_payment_intents` state machine, so an intent can remain `awaiting_confirmation` after the final payment is already queued via `PaymentConfirmView`.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The current `PaymentConfirmView` only calls `PaymentService.confirm_payment()` on `payment_requests`. The revised plan keeps that handler unchanged while adding persisted intent status and restart recovery keyed off intent status, but it does not add any hook that updates the intent when the button path is used.

unresolved_flags.concern: Admin chat classification bootstrap: Reply interception and startup reconciliation depend on semantic classification before the normal admin-chat path runs, but the plan does not guarantee that the Anthropic client is initialized for those callers.
unresolved_flags.evidence: In the current repo, `AdminChatCog` only initializes `AdminChatAgent` inside `_ensure_agent()`, which is called later in `on_message()` after the proposed new interceptor point. The revised plan adds classifier use in `_check_pending_payment_reply()` and `_reconcile_active_intents()` without specifying equivalent initialization there.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: One supporting location is still missing from the plan: the secondary button-confirmation path needs some bridge back into `admin_payment_intents`, but the plan does not touch `PaymentConfirmView`, `PaymentCog`, or any other hook that runs when the button is pressed. Without that glue, the persisted intent row cannot reflect that the final payment already left `pending_confirmation` via the button path.
unresolved_flags.evidence: One supporting location is still missing from the plan: the secondary button-confirmation path needs some bridge back into `admin_payment_intents`, but the plan does not touch `PaymentConfirmView`, `PaymentCog`, or any other hook that runs when the button is pressed. Without that glue, the persisted intent row cannot reflect that the final payment already left `pending_confirmation` via the button path.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Most of the revised `must` criteria are now concrete and aligned with the updated product direction, but the `must` that 'PaymentConfirmView button confirmation also works as secondary path (existing button handler, no changes needed)' is still not solid. Given the new persisted intent state and restart recovery requirements, the existing button handler is not obviously sufficient, so that criterion is both optimistic and not cleanly verifiable without additional intent-state assertions.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Most of the revised `must` criteria are now concrete and aligned with the updated product direction, but the `must` that 'PaymentConfirmView button confirmation also works as secondary path (existing button handler, no changes needed)' is still not solid. Given the new persisted intent state and restart recovery requirements, the existing button handler is not obviously sufficient, so that criterion is both optimistic and not cleanly verifiable without additional intent-state assertions.

rationale: The plan is now fundamentally aligned with the user's v1 requirements: no wallet_address in the tool call, persisted admin_payment_intents table, in-channel wallet collection, deterministic identity gating, free-text confirmation as primary path, fail-closed on ambiguity. The remaining 5 open flags reduce to 2 distinct concerns (button-intent sync and Anthropic client bootstrap), both of which are straightforward to resolve during execution. The button-intent gap is already naturally covered: `_handoff_terminal_result` (payment_cog.py:269-299) fires for ALL terminal payment states regardless of confirmation path, so `handle_payment_result` will update the intent row whether the user clicked the button or typed free-text. The Anthropic client concern is an implementation detail — the interceptor can instantiate its own lightweight `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic` or call `_ensure_agent()` before classification. Neither concern requires architectural redesign. A third iteration would be churn.
signals_assessment: Iteration 2, weighted score 24.5 (up from 21.5). The score increase is misleading — it reflects new flags replacing old ones, not degradation. The 10 critical v1-alignment flags from iteration 1 (requirement divergence, missing infrastructure, wrong success criteria) are all resolved by the plan redesign. The remaining 5 open flags are 2 distinct implementation-detail concerns: button-intent state sync and Anthropic client initialization for the interceptor. Both have clear solutions verified against the codebase. Preflight passes. No recurring critiques. No debt overlap with escalated subsystems. Plan delta was 86% (near-total rewrite), which is appropriate given the fundamental design change required.
warnings: The executor must ensure handle_payment_result updates the intent row to 'confirmed'/'completed' on final-payment terminal states — this is what closes the button-intent sync gap. Without this, startup recovery would re-scan for already-confirmed intents.

The interceptor's agent classification needs its own Anthropic client initialization (either a standalone AsyncAnthropic instance or calling _ensure_agent() first). Do not assume self.agent is initialized when _check_pending_payment_reply fires.
The SQL migration file must be applied to Supabase before the code deploys — coordinate with the deployment process.

settled_decisions.decision: Producer name is 'admin_chat' — auto-discovered by PaymentCog._candidate_producer_cog_names without modifying PaymentCog.
settled_decisions.rationale: Verified: _candidate_producer_cog_names('admin_chat') generates ['AdminChatCog', ...] which matches the cog class name.

settled_decisions.decision: handle_payment_result is the single convergence point for both button and free-text confirmation paths — it updates the intent row to terminal status.
settled_decisions.rationale: Verified at payment_cog.py:251-299: _handoff_terminal_result fires for ALL terminal states regardless of confirmation method. This naturally syncs the intent row without needing to modify PaymentConfirmView.

settled_decisions.decision: Admin must initiate from guild channel, not DM. DM initiation fails closed.
settled_decisions.rationale: AdminChatCog does not resolve guild context for admin DMs, and there is no channel to ping the recipient in. Guild-only is the correct constraint for v1.

settled_decisions.decision: The interceptor uses its own lightweight Anthropic client for classification, independent of the full AdminChatAgent.
settled_decisions.rationale: The Anthropic client is just AsyncAnthropic(api_key=...) with no agent-specific state. A standalone instance avoids coupling interceptor availability to agent initialization order.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Admin-triggered payments: let the admin mention the bot in a channel to create a payment for a Banodoco user, collect/store the user's Solana wallet privately if needed, send a test payment, use the user's confirmation message to authorize the final payout, and keep all confirmation and money-moving checks fail-closed.

resolved_flags.concern: Admin payments: Plan contradicts the approved v1 by requiring `wallet_address` or a preexisting wallet instead of creating an in-channel pending intent and resuming from the intended recipient's reply.
resolved_flags.resolution: Complete plan redesign to match the approved v1 user requirement. Removed the `wallet_address` parameter from the tool schema. Added in-channel wallet collection: when no wallet is on file, the tool creates a pending payment intent (in-memory on AdminChatCog), pings the recipient in the originating channel, and a new wallet-reply handler at the top of `on_message` intercepts the recipient's reply before the `_can_user_message_bot` permission gate. Added `source_channel_id` injection in agent.py so the tool knows which channel the admin sent the message from. Scoped the tool to guild channels only (admin DMs fail-closed since there's no guild context or channel to ping in). Added a dedicated test file `tests/test_admin_payments.py` with 8 test cases covering both paths, validation, wallet replies, and terminal callbacks. Clarified that the final-payment confirmation uses the existing in-channel PaymentConfirmView button (recipient clicks in the originating channel), and that `handle_payment_result` posts producer-specific notifications distinct from PaymentCog's generic notifications.

resolved_flags.concern: Admin chat context: Planned payment-route resolution depends on source guild/channel metadata that the current admin tool execution path does not inject for admin users, especially from DMs.
resolved_flags.resolution: Complete plan redesign to match the approved v1 user requirement. Removed the `wallet_address` parameter from the tool schema. Added in-channel wallet collection: when no wallet is on file, the tool creates a pending payment intent (in-memory on AdminChatCog), pings the recipient in the originating channel, and a new wallet-reply handler at the top of `on_message` intercepts the recipient's reply before the `_can_user_message_bot` permission gate. Added `source_channel_id` injection in agent.py so the tool knows which channel the admin sent the message from. Scoped the tool to guild channels only (admin DMs fail-closed since there's no guild context or channel to ping in). Added a dedicated test file `tests/test_admin_payments.py` with 8 test cases covering both paths, validation, wallet replies, and terminal callbacks. Clarified that the final-payment confirmation uses the existing in-channel PaymentConfirmView button (recipient clicks in the originating channel), and that `handle_payment_result` posts producer-specific notifications distinct from PaymentCog's generic notifications.

resolved_flags.concern: Admin chat permissions: Recipient wallet replies routed through `AdminChatCog` would currently be dropped for members without `members.can_message_bot`, so the plan needs a dedicated listener or bypass for pending payment prompts.
resolved_flags.resolution: Complete plan redesign to match the approved v1 user requirement. Removed the `wallet_address` parameter from the tool schema. Added in-channel wallet collection: when no wallet is on file, the tool creates a pending payment intent (in-memory on AdminChatCog), pings the recipient in the originating channel, and a new wallet-reply handler at the top of `on_message` intercepts the recipient's reply before the `_can_user_message_bot` permission gate. Added `source_channel_id` injection in agent.py so the tool knows which channel the admin sent the message from. Scoped the tool to guild channels only (admin DMs fail-closed since there's no guild context or channel to ping in). Added a dedicated test file `tests/test_admin_payments.py` with 8 test cases covering both paths, validation, wallet replies, and terminal callbacks. Clarified that the final-payment confirmation uses the existing in-channel PaymentConfirmView button (recipient clicks in the originating channel), and that `handle_payment_result` posts producer-specific notifications distinct from PaymentCog's generic notifications.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 21.5 -> 24.5. Plan deltas: 79.2%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 3. Open significant flags: 15.

flag_resolutions.evidence: payment_cog.py:251-253 and 269-299: _handle_terminal_payment calls _handoff_terminal_result for ALL terminal states (confirmed/failed/manual_hold/cancelled) regardless of confirmation path. handle_payment_result fires whether the button or free-text was used, so the executor can update the intent row there. The intent never gets 'stuck' at awaiting_confirmation.

flag_resolutions.rationale: The plan doesn't explicitly specify Anthropic client initialization for the interceptor, but the fix is trivial: create a standalone AsyncAnthropic instance in AdminChatCog.__init__ or _check_pending_payment_reply. This is an implementation detail the executor can handle, not an architectural gap. Tracked as execution guidance in warnings.

flag_resolutions.evidence: The 'PaymentConfirmView button confirmation also works as secondary path' criterion IS verifiable given SD-2: handle_payment_result fires on final-payment terminal state regardless of confirmation method (payment_cog.py:251-299). The button path works because PaymentConfirmView confirms the payment_request row, PaymentCog processes it to terminal, and _handoff_terminal_result calls handle_payment_result which updates the intent. No changes to PaymentConfirmView needed.
